title: [InstSimplify][InstCombine] Remove unnecessary `m_c_*` matchers.
summary: The developers generalized the optimization by transitioning from commutative matchers (`m_c_Add`, `m_c_And`, `m_c_Mul`) to non-commutative ones (`m_Add`, `m_And`, `m_Mul`). This enforces a canonical operand order (e.g., constant as the second operand for `add`), ensuring transformations apply consistently and avoid redundant matches. The strategy relies on prior canonicalization passes to arrange operands predictably, simplifying pattern logic and preventing infinite loops. For similar optimizations, standardize on canonical forms early and use non-commutative matchers to reduce complexity and ensure reliable transformations.
